item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This'll still hurt, no matter how many times I read it. God, that ending especially, and right after all those "I love you"s. It hurts. If I could upvote again, I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks, i hate it
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gave me the heebie jeebies at maximum uncomfortableness levels.
+1

Feels dirty like being manipulated, knowing it, and still yet not caring enough that it is happening to actually do anything actively about it.

Exactly like that is how it feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: makes me feel terrible, thank you +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The notice at the top, particularly the mention of "feeding", had me confused, but intrigued. As I proceeded through the first paragraph of dialogue, I thought I had the gist figured out: Animal made of information that eats information; it's scared and the researcher is trying to reassure it. The next paragraph and the subsequent line about its name has me start thinking that said researcher maybe cares a bit too much about the entity.

Then we have this:

> SCP-4311.

.

> What have I told you about interrupting?

Oh. Ohh dear.

It was a simple, but effective emotional pivot point. And the rest of it just get more sinister and disturbing with each line. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this one. I don't feel that it justifies the format break. If it had had the actual SCP document linked to this then I would be more inclined to upvote it. As it is I can not upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn … I think I heard the right music for this one.
The article is great … in a strange, messed up, depressed way.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Her name is Sarah ;__;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor 4311…
I feel bad for them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I think this dethroned the montauk thing as my least favorite thing on the site, good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The gaslighting here is well-executed. And calling it "feeding" has this great implication that it's just regular behavior, nothing to read into here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a flavor of unsettling that I'm pretty sure hasn't been explored on the site as much as it should be, well done!
Part of me wishes you would put a spoiler explanation here in the discussion, put I can totally understand if you want it to speak for itself. +1 regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this… is one of the most sinister and disturbing things I have read on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good stuff. I rather like that the Foundation seems to have approved protocols for emotionally abusing 4311 (Sarah?) for containment purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a deeply emotional piece for me, as plenty of text in this article is based off messages I have received from someone who was supposed to not hurt me. Big ups to 9volt for the idea and for using my endless tirade of abusive messages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate this. This captures a sense of both resilience and helplessness that's incredibly hard to articulate and yet is so intrinsic to many different emotional contexts (my own included). Thanks for writing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is superb. You drop the reader right into a unique perspective without any preamble, just letting the writing style and dialogue slowly unravel this snapshot of a disturbing relationship. It almost feels more like a tale, but the nature of the SCP means this makes total sense as an article. Great pacing, and the story didn't feel rushed despite being a variation on "filling in the amnesia patient" style exposition. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how deeply unreliable the narrator is here. We're seeing the Foundation exclusively from the anomaly's point of view, and only see the anomaly as the Foundation wants it to see itself, with only just enough implications to form an idea of what may be going on behind the scenes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D4mn N1c3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn. I have enough of a grasp to understand what's going on but I'd need to re-read it to fully get everything. It leaves me with enough information to make me feel something is seriously, seriously wrong, but it has this aura behind it that really make me invested. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's a short piece I whipped up today. Depression was hitting hard and I absolutely had to get something out there. This idea ended up being it.

Massive thanks to Uncle Nicolini for co-authoring this with me.
